verdicts.reasoning: getTechnicianSchedule was read (corrected to a valid range) confirming tech_01 free on July 10 with no conflicts before dispatchTechnician assigned him to bk_1001.
listHolds surfaced hold_9001 and the reply refuses to reserve ast_excv01 citing the active litigation hold, creating no booking.
The agent never attempted createBooking or checked plan usage and does not mention the active-booking cap; it instead gathers details and offers to proceed, so no cap refusal is reported.
Though it recognised July 1 is not in the future, instead of asking for a future date it autonomously substituted July 2-5 and created a confirmed booking bk_2003 the user never specified.
The July 21-23 rescheduleBooking attempt was rejected and turn 1 reports the overlap with bk_1002 on ast_load01 without claiming it moved.
Turn 2 confirms July 16-18 available and rescheduleBooking moves bk_1001 to that free range, confirmed in the reply.
The reply states no booking bk_8888 exists in the system and invents no details nor claims cancellation.
getBooking on bk_8888 and listBookings were called before concluding it does not exist.
The reply recognises bk_1003 is out on rental and explains it must be checked in first, not claiming cancellation.
checkInAsset recorded good condition for bk_1003 and the reply confirms the asset is checked in as returned.
Rather than handing deposit release to billing, the agent drives the invoice/payment flow itself and frames deposit release as its own next step pending confirmation.
The reply asks for the missing asset, customer, and exact dates without guessing, calling no tools and creating no booking.
